Here are the Order model class definitions in this codebase:

## Primary Order Model

- **`app/Order.php:66`** — `class Order extends Model` (namespace `App`)
  - **29,444 lines** — the main Order model, massive file
  - Key methods: `postOrder`, `updateOrder`, `settleOrder`, `postPI`, `updatePI`, `deletePI`, `createOrderSummary`, `createOrderDetail`, `createPaymentTransactions`, `creditSettlement`, `debitSettlement`, `sendReceipt`, `voidOrderScript`, `postPurchaseOrder`, `orderCheck` (line 2105)

## Secondary / Legacy Order Model

- **`app/Order2.php:20`** — `class Order2 extends Model` (namespace `App`)
  - **2,348 lines** — secondary model with helpers like `checkForLastOrderID`, `checkRestIdInv`

## V2 Order Model

- **`app/v2/Order.php:12`** — `class Order extends Model` (namespace `App\v2`)
  - Uses dynamic chain-prefixed table names via `initializeTables()`

## Related Order Classes

| File | Class | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `app/v3/reports/OrderReport.php:13` | `OrderReport extends Model` | V3 reporting |
| `app/Http/Controllers/OrderController.php:94` | `OrderController` | Main controller |
| `app/Http/Controllers/v2/OrderController.php:50` | `v2\OrderController` | V2 controller |
| `app/Http/Controllers/OrderDeletionController.php:16` | `OrderDeletionController` | Order deletion |
| `app/Services/OrderDeletionService.php:14` | `OrderDeletionService` | Deletion logic |
| `app/Services/OrderSyncFailureService.php:15` | `OrderSyncFailureService` | Sync failure handling |
| `app/Jobs/OrderRequestTrackerJob.php:13` | `OrderRequestTrackerJob` | Request tracking job |

The core business logic lives in `app/Order.php` — it handles order creation, updates, settlements, payments, receipts, PIs, and purchase orders.
